Obese travelers must buy extra seats if they encroach on adjacent space—Air Peace provides mobility aid but follows general airline rules on this.

I am reading in the airline instructions to disabled passengers that "you may make a special declaration of interest in relation to your mobility aid under Article 22(2) of the Montreal Convention," which just reeks of international intrigue.

@WarmanBill @GMB @sophmorgTV Wheelchairs being broken by an airline is damaged caused by someone else. So they should pay for it. But they don’t. They do if they break your suitcase though. Mobility aid when your legs don’t work = your legs, it is the same as your legs being broken.

@broadwaybabyto I’m a wheelchair user when flying longer distances. That’s the instruction of the airline. I have mobility issues after a brain haemorrhage. I try to use my legs as much as possible with aid of walking stick
